debug_info.goal:

R : ℕ → Ω → ℝ

inst✝⁴ : IsProbabilityMeasure P

ν : Kernel α ℝ
inst✝³ : IsMarkovKernel ν
inst✝² : StandardBorelSpace Ω
inst✝¹ : Nonempty Ω
inst✝ : Countable α
h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P
⊢ iIndepFun (fun p => rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) (P.prod (Bandit.streamMeasure ν))

      -- Use the fact that the rewardByCount is independent of itself at different times
      have h₃ : ∀ (m n : ℕ), iIndepFun (fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + m))

        intro m n
        -- Prove that the rewardByCount is independent of itself at different times
        sorry

have hA := h.measurable_A
  have hR := h.measurable_R
  simp only [funext_iff, Prod.mk.injEq] at *
  intro s t hs ht
  have h₁ : ∀ (i : ℕ), i ∈ s → i ∈ t → False := by
    intro i hi₁ hi₂
    have h₂ : i ∈ s ∧ i ∈ t := ⟨hi₁, hi₂⟩
    have h₃ : s ∩ t = ∅ := by
      simp [Set.eq_empty_iff_forall_not_mem]
      <;>
      (try decide) <;>
      (try omega) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try simp_all [indepFun_nat_iff_forall_indepFun]) <;>
      (try norm_num) <;>
      (try aesop)
    have h₄ : i ∈ s ∩ t := Set.mem_of_mem_inter_left hi₁
    rw [h₃] at h₄
    exact Set.not_mem_empty _ h₄
  have h₂ : s ∩ t = ∅ := by
    apply Set.eq_empty_of_forall_not_mem
    intro x hx
    have h₃ : x ∈ s := Set.mem_of_mem_inter_left hx
    have h₄ : x ∈ t := Set.mem_of_mem_inter_right hx
    have h₅ : False := h₁ x h₃ h₄
    exact h₅.elim
  simp [h₂]
  <;>
  (try decide) <;>
  (try omega) <;>
  (try aesop) <;>
  (try simp_all [indepFun_nat_iff_forall_indepFun]) <;>
  (try norm_num) <;>

have h₁ : ∀ (p : α × ℕ), rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1) = rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1) := by intro p; rfl
  have h₂ : iIndepFun (fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) 𝔓' := by
    have h₃ : iIndepFun (fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) 𝔓' := by
      -- Use the fact that the rewardByCount function is independent for different indices
      have h₄ : iIndepFun (fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) 𝔓' := by
        -- Prove the independence using the properties of the sequence and the Markov kernel
        have h₅ : iIndepFun (fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) 𝔓' := by
          -- Use the previously established iIndepFun properties
          have h₆ : iIndepFun (fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) 𝔓' := by
            -- Prove the independence using the properties of the sequence and the Markov kernel
            exact by
              -- Use the fact that the rewardByCount function is independent for different indices
              have h₇ : iIndepFun (fun (n : ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R a (n + 1)) 𝔓' := iIndepFun_rewardByCount' h a
              -- Use the previously established iIndepFun properties to conclude the proof
              simpa [Prod.ext_iff] using h₇
          exact h₆
        exact h₅
      exact h₄
    exact h₃
  exact h₂

have hA := h.measurable_A
  have hR := h.measurable_R
  have h' : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P := h
  have h₂ : ∀ (a : α) (n : ℕ),
      (rewardByCount A R a (n + 1)) ⟂ᵢ[𝔓']
        fun ω (i : Iic n) ↦ rewardByCount A R a i ω := by
    intro a n
    have h₃ := indepFun_rewardByCount_Iic h a n
    simpa using h₃
  -- Use the given condition to prove the main statement
  have h₃ : iIndepFun (fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) 𝔓' := by
    have h₄ : ∀ m : ℕ, iIndepFunBelow ⟨m, by
      -- Prove that the function is iIndepFunBelow m
      intro n
      have h₅ := h₂ (Classical.arbitrary α) m
      simp_all [iIndepFunBelow,RewardByCount]
      <;>
      (try simp_all [iIndepFunBelow, RewardByCount]) <;>
      (try norm_num) <;>
      (try ring_nf) <;>
      (try omega) <;>
      (try aesop)
      ⟩ (fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) 𝔓' := by
      intro m
      apply iIndepFunBelow.mono _
      <;> simp [iIndepFunBelow]
      <;>
      (try simp_all [iIndepFunBelow, RewardByCount]) <;>
      (try norm_num) <;>
      (try ring_nf) <;>
      (try omega) <;>
      (try aesop)
    -- Use the above result to prove the main statement
    have h₅ : iIndepFun (fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) 𝔓' := by
      refine iIndepFun_of_forall_iIndepFunBelow _
      intro m
      exact h₄ m
    exact h₅
  exact h₃

have h₁ : iIndepFun (fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) 𝔓' := by
    have h₂ : ∀ (a : α) (n : ℕ),
      (rewardByCount A R a (n + 1)) ⟂ᵢ[𝔓'] fun ω (i : Iic n) ↦ rewardByCount A R a i := by
      intro a n
      exact indepFun_rewardByCount_Iic h a n
    -- Use the fact that each reward is independent of the previous ones
    -- to show the iIndepFun property for the sequence of rewards.
    have h₃ : iIndepFun (fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) 𝔓' := by
      -- Use the fact that each reward is independent of the previous ones
      -- to show the iIndepFun property for the sequence of rewards.
      have h₄ : iIndepFun (fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) 𝔓' := by
        -- Use the fact that each reward is independent of the previous ones
        -- to show the iIndepFun property for the sequence of rewards.
        exact iIndepFun_of_indepFun_seq (fun n ↦
          (iIndepFun_congr (fun p => p.2) (by
            simp [Function.funext_iff, IdentDistrib]
            <;>
            simp_all [IdentDistrib]
            <;>
            try aesop
            )
          )
          (by
            have h₅ := h₂ (Classical.arbitrary α) n
            simp_all [iIndepFun]
          )
        )
      exact h₄
    exact h₃
  exact h₁
